docs.title: 3 Steps Toward Growing the Funds for Restoration
docs.file_name: 3 Steps Toward Growing the Funds for Restoration _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI commentary laying out three steps to grow private capital for landscape restoration: build a pipeline of investable projects, expand the investor base, and aggregate projects into funds and securities.

docs.title: Food Systems at Risk: Transformative Adaptation for Long-Term Food Security
docs.file_name: TACR Issue Brief Funders.pdf
docs.short_summary: A funder-targeted companion brief to the WRI report Food Systems at Risk, laying out specific actions that adaptation funders (GCF, MDBs, bilaterals, philanthropy) should take to support transformative agricultural adaptation.

docs.title: Mexico PES Policy Brief
docs.file_name: Mexico PES Policy Brief_V2.docx
docs.short_summary: A policy brief analyzing Mexico's national PES system, run by CONAFOR since 2003, covering three federal schemes and drawing lessons for countries designing PES at scale.

docs.title: The Economic Costs and Benefits of Securing Community Forest Tenure: Evidence from Brazil and Guatemala
docs.file_name: 15_WP_CLUA_Forest_Tenure.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI working paper presenting benefit-cost analyses of community forest tenure security in Brazil and Guatemala, finding benefits outweigh costs in both study areas.

docs.title: Climate Benefits, Tenure Costs: The Economic Case For Securing Indigenous Land Rights in the Amazon
docs.file_name: Climate_Benefits_Tenure_Costs.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI report making the economic case for securing Indigenous land rights in the Amazon, using matching analysis in Bolivia, Brazil, and Colombia to quantify avoided deforestation, carbon benefits, and other ecosystem service values.

docs.title: A Guide to the Restoration Opportunities Assessment Methodology (ROAM): Assessing Forest Landscape Restoration Opportunities at the National or Sub-National Level
docs.file_name: A guide to the Restoration Opportunities Assessment Methodology (ROAM).pdf
docs.short_summary: The foundational handbook for the Restoration Opportunities Assessment Methodology (ROAM), jointly published by IUCN and WRI to guide national and sub-national assessments of FLR opportunities.

docs.title: Cultivating networks and mapping social landscapes: How to understand restoration governance in Rwanda
docs.file_name: Cultivating networks and mapping social landscapes - How to understand restoration governance in Rwanda.pdf
docs.short_summary: A peer-reviewed Land Use Policy article applying social network analysis to restoration governance in Rwanda, mapping actor relationships across information, finance, and seedling flows at national, district, and community scales.

docs.title: Text mining policy: Classifying forest and landscape restoration policy agenda with neural information retrieval
docs.file_name: Text mining policy - Classifying forest and landscape restoration policy agenda with neural information retrieval.pdf
docs.short_summary: A KDD 2019 FEED workshop paper by John Brandt (WRI) introducing an unsupervised neural information retrieval architecture to classify FLR policy agenda across policy documents in Malawi, Kenya, and Rwanda, achieving F1 of 0.83 across 14 policy agenda.

docs.title: Taking to Scale Tree-Based Systems that Enhance Food Security, Improve Resilience to Climate Change, and Sequester Carbon in Malawi
docs.file_name: Taking to Scale tree based sytstem- Malawi.pdf
docs.short_summary: A Malawi country case study on scaling farmer-managed natural regeneration (FMNR) and tree-based agricultural systems to enhance food security, climate resilience, and carbon sequestration, co-authored by ICRAF, WRI, and the World Bank-housed PROFOR program.

docs.title: Forest restoration projects could create billions in new revenue
docs.file_name: Forest restoration projects could create billions in new revenue _ Trellis.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 2017 Trellis (formerly GreenBiz) article by Andrew Wu framing landscape restoration for mainstream sustainability-business readers, arguing restoration generates both recurring income and capital gains with a total economic opportunity of ~$84 billion.

docs.title: 3 Ways Well-Intentioned Forest Laws and Their Enforcement Can Block Restoration
docs.file_name: 3 Ways Well-Intentioned Forest Laws and Their Enforcement Can Block Restoration _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI commentary on how rigid forest laws, unclear tenure, and lax enforcement can create paradoxes that undermine restoration — using a Guatemalan landholder example to illustrate.

docs.title: 4 Ways Farmers Can Adapt to Climate Change and Generate Income
docs.file_name: 4 Ways Farmers Can Adapt to Climate Change and Generate Income _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI commentary drawing on a new WRI Brasil / GIZ working paper to present four sustainable agricultural practices — integrated crop-livestock-forestry, agroforestry, direct seeding, and rainwater harvesting — that build climate resilience while generating farmer income.

docs.title: 6 Barriers to Protecting and Restoring Forests – and Strategies to Overcome Them
docs.file_name: 6 Barriers to Protecting and Restoring Forests – and Strategies to Overcome Them _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A companion commentary to WRI's Public-Sector Measures working paper (commissioned by the Food and Land Use Coalition), identifying six economic and political-economy barriers to scaling forest conservation and restoration, with overcoming strategies.

docs.title: A Tale of Two Cities in Brazil (and the Forest that Connects Them)
docs.file_name: A Tale of Two Cities in Brazil (and the Forest that Connects Them) _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary profiling how two very different Brazilian cities — Salvador and São Paulo — are working to protect and restore the Atlantic Forest, the country's other (and more endangered) rainforest.

docs.title: African Nations Poised to Rapidly Accelerate Landscape Restoration
docs.file_name: African Nations Poised to Rapidly Accelerate Landscape Restoration _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary marking AFR100's first anniversary, reporting that 21 African nations had committed 63.3 million hectares of land to restoration and catalyzing $481 million in private sector funding plus $1 billion in development finance.

docs.title: Aligning Ambitions: The Case for Including Restoration Targets in Climate Goals
docs.file_name: Aligning Ambitions_ The Case for Including Restoration Targets in Climate Goals _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI commentary arguing countries should align their Bonn Challenge restoration commitments with their Nationally Determined Contributions under the Paris Agreement — noting only 43 of 137 NDCs include numerical carbon targets for restoration.

docs.title: Attention Investors: African Entrepreneurs Are Restoring Land and Making Profit
docs.file_name: Attention Investors_ African Entrepreneurs Are Restoring Land and Making Profit _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary profiling Kenyan restoration entrepreneurs (KOFAR, Lentera Africa) to illustrate the 'missing middle' problem — restoration businesses seeking $20k-$200k in financing find few investors in that range.

docs.title: Can We Restore 350 Million Hectares by 2030?
docs.file_name: Can We Restore 350 Million Hectares by 2030_ _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary arguing that the 350 Mha NY Declaration target can only be met if restoration shifts from tree-planting-at-scale toward protecting and managing naturally regenerating trees, citing early successes in Niger, Ethiopia, and Costa Rica.

docs.title: Challenges of Measuring Tree Restoration
docs.file_name: Challenges of Measuring Tree Restoration _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary on the methodological challenges of tracking tree growth at global scale, in the context of the Trillion Trees initiative and corporate pledges — arguing that robust monitoring requires combining satellite data with ground-truthing.

docs.title: Counting Trees to Save the Woods: Using Big Data to Map Deforestation
docs.file_name: Counting Trees to Save the Woods_ Using Big Data to Map Deforestation _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary on using big-data/satellite approaches (Global Forest Watch, Collect Earth) to map deforestation and restoration at scale.

docs.title: Deforestation Threatens the Mekong, but New Trees Are Growing in Surprising Places
docs.file_name: Deforestation Threatens the Mekong, but New Trees Are Growing in Surprising Places _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary examining deforestation trends in the Mekong region — noting net losses inside forests but net gains in tree density in agricultural areas outside forests in all countries except Laos.

docs.title: Finance for Women Is Key to Restoring Africa’s Degraded Lands
docs.file_name: Finance for Women Is Key to Restoring Africa’s Degraded Lands _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A vignette profiling the Kenyan Paran Women Group (a collective of 64 Indigenous women's groups) to argue that inclusive, women-led restoration finance is key to restoring Africa's degraded lands.

docs.title: Forests Are in the Paris Agreement! Now What?
docs.file_name: Forests Are in the Paris Agreement! Now What_ _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary on the inclusion of REDD+ in the Paris Agreement (Article 5), covering what the political signal means for forest finance and what comes next operationally.

docs.title: Growing Trees and Growing Profit: Is Your Business a “Restoration Enterprise?”
docs.file_name: Growing Trees and Growing Profit_ Is Your Business a “Restoration Enterprise_” _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary defining 'restoration enterprise' — companies whose value proposition is linked directly or indirectly to restoration — and introducing examples from EcoPlanet Bamboo to BioCarbon Engineering.

docs.title: Growing Trees the Right Way with TerraMatch
docs.file_name: Growing Trees the Right Way with TerraMatch _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A vignette introducing TerraMatch — WRI's platform for connecting restoration investors with trusted local tree-growing partners — using the Turkey 11M-saplings failure and 1970s Kenya mesquite introduction as cautionary tales.

docs.title: How Brazil, Panama and Costa Rica Breathed New Life Into Their Degraded Lands
docs.file_name: How Brazil, Panama and Costa Rica Breathed New Life Into Their Degraded Lands _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: Third installment of WRI's Restoration Global Tour series, profiling restoration success stories in Brazil (Tijuca urban forest), Panama, and Costa Rica — drawing on case studies from WRI's Restoration Diagnostic.

docs.title: How Can Restoring Degraded Landscapes Deliver Financial Returns?
docs.file_name: How Can Restoring Degraded Landscapes Deliver Financial Returns_ _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A short explainer arguing restoration projects deliver returns in two forms — income (recurring revenue) and capital gains (land value appreciation) — with examples from Indonesia and North Carolina.

docs.title: How To Monitor Restoration With Collect Earth
docs.file_name: How To Monitor Restoration With Collect Earth _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI Insights Finding on how local stakeholders can use Collect Earth (FAO's visual interpretation tool pulling Google Earth imagery) to map trees that escape satellite algorithms, especially in dry forests and on-farm landscapes.

docs.title: How to Effectively Restore Landscapes in 3 Steps
docs.file_name: How to Effectively Restore Landscapes in 3 Steps _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: An explainer distilling three steps for effective landscape restoration: (1) understand the state of the land and plan with people, (2) set realistic targets and build capacity, (3) monitor and learn.

docs.title: In a Small Amazonian Community, Agroforestry Takes Off
docs.file_name: In a Small Amazonian Community, Agroforestry Takes Off _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A profile of Marliane das Chagas Soares and 21 other families in the Amazonian community of Juruti, Pará, Brazil, transitioning from cassava monoculture / slash-and-burn to diversified agroforestry with WRI Brasil support.

docs.title: Learning from African Farmers: How “Re-greening” Boosts Food Security; Curbs Climate Change
docs.file_name: Learning from African Farmers_ How “Re-greening” Boosts Food Security; Curbs Climate Change _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: An early (2013) WRI commentary by Chris Reij tied to President Obama's Africa trip, introducing farmer-managed re-greening in the Sahel as a low-cost, scalable strategy for food security and climate action.

docs.title: Learnings from TerraFund’s 100 Land Restoration Projects in Africa
docs.file_name: Learnings from TerraFund’s 100 Land Restoration Projects in Africa _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A project-update article reporting on two years of TerraFund for AFR100's 100-project cohort (launched 2021 with Bezos Earth Fund and partners) — $15M invested across 27 countries, 12.5M trees grown, 36,000 people paid, 202,000 beneficiaries.

docs.title: Multi-stakeholder Platforms Can Help Landscape Restoration Initiatives
docs.file_name: Multi-stakeholder Platforms Can Help Landscape Restoration Initiatives _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI Technical Perspective on multi-stakeholder platforms (MSPs) for landscape restoration, reporting on stocktake convenings in Rwanda and Malawi within the AFR100 context (12 of 34 AFR100 countries have MSPs).

docs.title: Radio Helps Niger Farmers Bring Life Back to Their Land
docs.file_name: Radio Helps Niger Farmers Bring Life Back to Their Land _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A Taking Root series profile of Ali Malam and Sakina Mati, Nigerien farmers using local radio to share FMNR success stories with other farmers — illustrating peer-to-peer diffusion as a low-cost restoration scaling mechanism.

docs.title: Redefining Business with Environmental Restoration
docs.file_name: Redefining Business with Environmental Restoration _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary introducing The Business of Planting Trees report (co-authored with TNC), profiling 14 of 140 researched restoration businesses — from EcoPlanet Bamboo to BioCarbon Engineering's drone tree-planting to Tentree's apparel-linked planting.

docs.title: Restoration: One of the Most Overlooked Opportunities for Economic Growth
docs.file_name: Restoration_ One of the Most Overlooked Opportunities for Economic Growth _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary framing WRI's Roots of Prosperity report, arguing restoration delivers $7-30 in benefits per $1 invested and identifying five government levers for unlocking finance (carbon pricing, incentive shifts, risk mitigation, land use planning, rigorous accounting).

docs.title: Safeguarding the Carbon Stored in Indigenous and Community Lands is Essential to Meeting Climate Goals
docs.file_name: Safeguarding the Carbon Stored in Indigenous and Community Lands is Essential to Meeting Climate Goals _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary introducing a joint RRI/Woods Hole/WRI/EDF report showing Indigenous peoples and local communities manage at least 293,061 MtC — 17% of total forestland carbon globally — and that protecting their land rights is essential to meeting climate goals.

docs.title: The Restoration Revolution
docs.file_name: The Restoration Revolution _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary on the global restoration movement, discussing the use of degraded land for agricultural expansion (instead of deforestation of forested land) as a tool for food security, climate action, and rural livelihoods.

docs.title: The Roadmap to Decarbonization Won’t Go Far without Land
docs.file_name: The Roadmap to Decarbonization Won’t Go Far without Land _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary on a decarbonization roadmap published in Science arguing that the agriculture and forestry sector was under-specified despite being critical to achieving net-zero — and laying out four concrete land-sector actions by 2050.

docs.title: To Regreen Rwanda, Empower Farmers. Here Are 5 Ways to Begin
docs.file_name: To Regreen Rwanda, Empower Farmers. Here Are 5 Ways to Begin _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A profile of Rwandan farmer Patricie Nyirabahinzi in Gatsibo District, growing native Vernonia amygdalina (bitterleaf) for traditional medicine livelihoods — illustrating how restoration programs miss native-species needs and five ways to center farmers.

docs.title: Tree-planting Startup Shows 4 Steps to Raising Capital for Restoration
docs.file_name: Tree-planting Startup Shows 4 Steps to Raising Capital for Restoration _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary introducing the Attracting Private Investment to Landscape Restoration working paper via a profile of Land Life Company, an Amsterdam restoration startup that raised Series A — and laying out the paper's four steps.

docs.title: Want to Grow Trees? Consider These 5 Lessons
docs.file_name: Want to Grow Trees_ Consider These 5 Lessons _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A commentary written in response to the 1t.org trillion-trees announcement, laying out five principles — start with people, pick the right species, invest in maintenance, monitor, conserve existing trees — with the Priceless Planet Coalition as example.

docs.title: What Does it Take for Successful Forest Landscape Restoration?
docs.file_name: What Does it Take for Successful Forest Landscape Restoration_ _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: An early (2013) WRI commentary tied to a Global Partnership on Forest Landscape Restoration meeting, introducing 'forest landscape restoration' and WRI/IUCN's Landscapes of Opportunity 2011 global assessment.

docs.title: Research Gaps and Priorities in Silviculture of Native Species in Brazil
docs.file_name: AF_WRI_WorkingPaper_ResearchGapsInSilviculture_digital_0.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI Brasil working paper identifying research gaps and priorities for silviculture of native species (Amazon and Atlantic Forest biomes), proposing four investment scenarios for a pre-competitive R&D Platform and estimating ROI for each.

docs.title: Attracting Private Investment to Landscape Restoration: A Roadmap
docs.file_name: Attracting_Private_Investment_to_Landscape_Restoration_-_A_Roadmap_0 (1).pdf
docs.short_summary: A 12-page WRI working paper providing a four-step practical roadmap for restoration enterprises to attract private capital: get to know investors, develop a business plan, set up an investable entity, track performance.

docs.title: Unasylva
docs.file_name: Bamboo_the_opportunities_for_forest_and.pdf
docs.short_summary: An entire issue of FAO's Unasylva international journal of forestry (Vol. 66, 2015/3), focused on forest and landscape restoration including a feature on bamboo's role as a restoration species.

docs.title: Costa Rica's Payment for Ecosystem Services Program
docs.file_name: Costa Rica PES Brief.docx
docs.short_summary: A WRI policy brief on Costa Rica's PPSA — the world's longest-standing national PES program (established 1996 under Forest Law 7575), which has protected or restored 1.1M+ hectares and distributed $565M+ in payments over three decades.

docs.title: Enriching lives and lands through Assisted Natural Regeneration: Financial analysis of enrichment planting of naturally regenerating forests and woodlands with native non-timber products
docs.file_name: Economics of ANR Report final draft.pdf
docs.region: Latin America and Caribbean | Sub-Saharan Africa | Southeast Asia
docs.short_summary: A 203-page financial analysis of 14 Assisted Natural Regeneration systems across Latin America, Africa, and Asia — focused on enrichment planting of naturally regenerating forests and woodlands with native non-timber forest product species.

docs.title: Guatemala PES Policy Brief
docs.file_name: Guatemala Policy Brief Final.docx
docs.short_summary: A WRI policy brief on Guatemala's three forestry incentive programs (PINFOR 1996-2016; PINPEP for smallholders since 2010; PROBOSQUE succeeding PINFOR through 2046) — which have invested $525M+ and enrolled ~588,000 ha of land.

docs.title: Identifying social media user demographics and topic diversity with computational social science: a case study of a major international policy forum
docs.file_name: Identifying social media user demographics and topic diversity with computational social science - a case study of a major international policy forum.pdf
docs.short_summary: A peer-reviewed paper (Journal of Computational Social Science 3:167-188) applying social network analysis and machine learning to Twitter data from the Global Landscapes Forum (Nairobi vs Bonn) to measure whether African, women, and young voices are elevated.

docs.title: Mapping Social Landscapes: A Guide to Identifying the Networks, Priorities, and Values of Restoration Actors
docs.file_name: MAPPING SOCIAL LANDSCAPES A Guide to Identifying the Networks, Priorities, and Values of Restoration Actors.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 96-page WRI guidebook operationalizing social network analysis (SNA) for restoration governance — mapping the connections, priorities, and values of actors in restoration landscapes at national, district, and community scales.

docs.title: Mapping together: A guide to monitoring forest and landscape restoration using Collect Earth mapathons
docs.file_name: MAPPING TOGETHER A Guide to Monitoring Forest and Landscape Restoration Using Collect Earth Mapathons.pdf
docs.short_summary: A joint FAO/WRI 98-page guidebook on monitoring forest and landscape restoration using Collect Earth mapathons — laying out eight operational steps from data use planning through results dissemination.

docs.title: MapTenure: Enabling Tenurial Clarity for Orange Areas of Central India
docs.file_name: MAPTENURE - ENABLING TENURIAL CLARITY FOR ORANGE AREAS OF CENTRAL INDIA.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI India Technical Note on MAPTenure — the first web-based platform for resolving 'orange area' tenure ambiguity in Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh, where 1.2M ha and 1.5M families are affected by overlapping revenue/forest department claims.

docs.title: Food Systems at Risk: Transformative Adaptation for Long-term Food Security
docs.file_name: TACR Issue Brief Policymakers.pdf
docs.short_summary: The policymaker-targeted companion to WRI's Food Systems at Risk report, detailing how governments and adaptation support organizations can integrate transformative adaptation into plans and policies.

docs.title: Food Systems at Risk: Transformative Adaptation for Long-Term Food Security
docs.file_name: TACR Issue Brief Researchers.pdf
docs.short_summary: The researcher-targeted companion to WRI's Food Systems at Risk report, summarizing key recommendations on how agricultural research organizations can expand their agendas to better incorporate transformative adaptation approaches.

docs.title: Tackling the challenges of assessing socioeconomic impacts of farmland restoration: The case of Malawi
docs.file_name: Tackling the challenges of assessing socioeconomic impacts of farmland restoration The case of Malawi.pdf
docs.short_summary: A peer-reviewed Ecological Indicators paper (148, 2023, 110068) using two-year panel data from Malawi's Integrated Household Panel Survey and a Difference-in-Difference model to estimate causal effects of farmland restoration on household socioeconomic outcomes.

docs.title: The Economic Case for Landscape Restoration in Latin America
docs.file_name: The_Economic_Case_for_Landscape_Restoration_in_Latin_America.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI report monetizing costs and benefits of restoring 20 million hectares in Latin America and the Caribbean — the Initiative 20x20 target. Net annual flow gains turn positive by year 9.

docs.title: The Role of Assisted Natural Regeneration in Accelerating Forest and Landscape Restoration
docs.file_name: assisted-natural-regeneration-accelerating-forest-landscape-restoration-EN_1.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 74-page WRI Brasil Practice Note on the role of Assisted Natural Regeneration in accelerating forest and landscape restoration, drawing on practical field experiences.

docs.title: The Business of Planting Trees: A Growing Investment Opportunity
docs.file_name: business-planting-trees_0.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 66-page joint WRI/TNC report profiling 14 restoration-linked businesses across four themes (technology, consumer products, project management, commercial forestry) — the foundational document for WRI's 'restoration enterprise' framing.

docs.title: Enhancing climate finance access for Indigenous peoples and local communities
docs.file_name: enhancing-climate-finance-access-indigenous-peoples-local-communities_0.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 120-page WRI report (May 2025) analyzing how to enhance climate finance access for Indigenous peoples and local communities through payments for ecosystem services — with deep case studies from Guatemala (INAB, Utz Che', FEDECOVERA).

docs.title: Financial Sector Guidebook on Nature-Based Solutions Investment: Aligning Investment With Impacts and Showcasing Examples
docs.file_name: financial-sector-guidebok-nature-based-solutions.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 92-page WRI Guidebook (June 2025) for the financial sector on Nature-Based Solutions investment, introducing the 'INSPIRE' framework for aligning investments with NBS opportunities and showcasing real-world examples.

docs.title: Food Systems at Risk: Transformative Adaptation for Long-Term Food Security
docs.file_name: food-systems-at-risk-transformative-adaptation-for-long-term-food-security.pdf
docs.short_summary: The WRI report underpinning the TACR Issue Brief series — examining how climate change impacts agriculture and why transformative (not just incremental) adaptation is needed to protect livelihoods of hundreds of millions of smallholder farmers and herders.

docs.title: Gender-Responsive Agricultural Adaptation
docs.file_name: gender-responsive-agricultural-adaptation.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 24-page WRI Working Paper (v1.1, October 2023) arguing that gender-responsive agricultural adaptation is chronically underfunded — even the amounts reported as gender-responsive are likely inflated due to accounting challenges.

docs.title: A Guide to Selecting Ecosystem Service Models for Decision-Making
docs.file_name: guide-selecting-ecosystem-service-model-decision-making_0.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 48-page WRI guide (January 2018) helping decision-makers in Sub-Saharan Africa select an appropriate ecosystem service model — covering InVEST, Co$ting Nature, WaterWorld, ARIES, and LPJ-GUESS — with a five-step selection process.

docs.title: Healing the Wounded Land: The Role of Public Economic Incentives in Scaling Up Restoration Efforts in Six Latin American Countries
docs.file_name: healing-wounded-land-EN.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI Issue Brief on the role of public economic incentives in scaling up restoration efforts across six Latin American countries — comparing incentive programs and distilling design recommendations.

docs.title: Improving Land and Water Management
docs.file_name: improving_land_and_water_management_0.pdf
docs.short_summary: Installment 4 of WRI's 'Creating a Sustainable Food Future' working paper series (October 2013) — profiling agroforestry, conservation agriculture, rainwater harvesting, and integrated soil fertility management for Sub-Saharan drylands.

docs.title: Investing in Native Tree Species and Agroforestry Systems in Brazil: An Economic Valuation
docs.file_name: investing-in-native-tree-species-and-agroforestry-systems-in-brazil-an-economic-valuation_0.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 96-page WRI Brasil economic valuation of investing in Brazilian native tree species and agroforestry systems — the VERENA Project's flagship document, introducing the VERENA Investment Tool.

docs.title: Public-Sector Measures to Conserve and Restore Forests: Overcoming Economic and Political Economy Barriers
docs.file_name: public-sector-measures-conserve-restore-forests.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 56-page WRI Working Paper commissioned by the Food and Land Use Coalition (FOLU) as part of its Growing Better report — identifying six economic and political economy barriers to scaling forest conservation and restoration, with strategies.

docs.title: Repurposing Agricultural Subsidies to Restore Degraded Farmland and Grow Rural Prosperity
docs.file_name: repurposing-agricultural-subsidies-restore-degraded-farmland-grow-rural-prosperity_3.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 56-page WRI Report on repurposing agricultural subsidies to restore degraded farmland and grow rural prosperity, with in-depth case studies from India, Brazil, and Mexico.

docs.title: Restoration Launchpad Guidebook
docs.file_name: restoration-launchpad-guidebook_0.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 84-page WRI step-by-step guidebook for landscape restoration planners and practitioners — synthesizing WRI's restoration methodology into an accessible launchpad format.

docs.title: Restoring Landscapes in India for Climate and Communities
docs.file_name: restoring-landscapes-india-climate-and-communities.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 104-page WRI India report on key findings from Sidhi district in Madhya Pradesh — combining field assessments, Collect Earth mapathons, and stakeholder consultations to diagnose restoration opportunities and barriers.

docs.title: The Road to Restoration
docs.file_name: road-to-restoration.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 78-page joint FAO/WRI guide to identifying priorities and indicators for monitoring forest and landscape restoration — the parent framework that the Mapping Together Collect Earth mapathon guide (ks_053) operationalizes.

docs.title: Roadmap for Scaling Trees Outside Forests in India: Learnings from Select States on Policy Incentives, Enabling Conditions, and Barriers
docs.file_name: roadmap-scaling-trees-outside-forests-india.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 52-page WRI India Working Paper mapping incentive policies, enabling conditions, and barriers for scaling Trees outside Forests (ToF) across six Indian states — Gujarat, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Odisha, Punjab, and Telangana.

docs.title: Roots of Prosperity: The Economics and Finance of Restoring Land
docs.file_name: roots-of-prosperity_0.pdf
docs.short_summary: Roots of Prosperity: The Economics and Finance of Restoring Land — the WRI flagship 80-page report on restoration finance, arguing every $1 invested generates $7-30 in economic benefits.

docs.title: Scaling Up Regreening: Six Steps to Success
docs.file_name: scaling-regreening-six-steps-success.pdf
docs.short_summary: Scaling Up Regreening: Six Steps to Success — the canonical Reij/Winterbottom 72-page practical guide to forest and landscape restoration based on evidence from the Sahel and beyond.

docs.title: Sustainability Index for Landscape Restoration
docs.file_name: sustainability-index-landscape-restoration.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 68-page WRI technical document introducing the Sustainability Index for Landscape Restoration (SILR) — a tool for monitoring the biophysical and socioeconomic impacts of landscape restoration, developed with El Salvador partners under German BMUB funding.

docs.title: The Global Land Squeeze: Managing the Growing Competition for Land
docs.file_name: the-global-land-squeeze-report.pdf
docs.short_summary: The Global Land Squeeze — a 176-page WRI flagship report (2023) by Searchinger et al. analyzing competing demands on global land between food, carbon, biodiversity, and construction wood, using the CHARM model to project GHG consequences of alternative pathways.

docs.title: AI Can Aid in Nature Restoration
docs.file_name: AI Can Aid in Nature Restoration _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI Insights commentary arguing that a new AI model (DINOv3-based remote sensing) can pinpoint where trees are regrowing and direct restoration finance to the most promising projects, illustrated with a Ghana mangrove case.

docs.title: Advancing Landscape and Seascape Restoration: The Case for Policy Coherence and Good Practice Sharing
docs.file_name: Advancing-LS-Restoration_The-Case-for-Policy-Coherence-and-Good-Practice-Sharing_25.pdf
docs.short_summary: A multi-organization policy brief making the case that policy coherence — across sectors, governance levels, and time — is a prerequisite for effective landscape and seascape restoration, with recommendations for governments, practitioners, and financiers.

docs.title: How Governments Can Reward Farmers for Conservation Efforts
docs.file_name: How Governments Can Reward Farmers for Conservation Efforts _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI commentary identifying seven ways governments can use investments and policies — including PES, technical assistance, and land tenure reform — to support farmer-led landscape restoration, anchored in a Guatemala / INAB case.

docs.title: How Restoring Degraded Forests Affects Temperature and Precipitation
docs.file_name: How Restoring Degraded Forests Affects Temperature and Precipitation _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI commentary on emerging evidence that forest restoration delivers non-carbon climate benefits — particularly cooling effects and rainfall regulation through evapotranspiration — and frames why this matters for restoration policy and practice.

docs.title: How to Measure, Report on and Verify Land Restoration Projects
docs.file_name: How to Measure, Report on and Verify Land Restoration Projects _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI Project Update introducing the TerraMatch / Restore Local approach to MRV for restoration: a unified framework for monitoring, reporting, and verification across hundreds of African TerraFund projects. Companion piece to the 2026 'Land restoration from planting to proof' guidebook.

docs.title: How to Scale Up Nature-based Solutions for Adaptation
docs.file_name: How to Scale Up Nature-based Solutions for Adaptation _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI Insights piece summarizing a study on how to scale up nature-based solutions for climate adaptation, surveying political momentum (Glasgow Declaration, Adaptation Fund pledges) and articulating opportunities across ecosystems.

docs.title: New Data Shows Where Latin America Is Gaining Trees
docs.file_name: New Data Shows Where Latin America Is Gaining Trees _ World Resources Institute.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI Insights piece presenting new University of Maryland satellite data on tree cover gains and losses across Latin America (2015–2023), assessing Initiative 20x20 progress at the country level five years out from 2030.

docs.title: Reforestation With Native Species: Case Studies, Economic Viability and Environmental Benefits
docs.file_name: Reforestation-with-native-species-case-studies-economic-viability-and-enviromental-benefits.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 2021 Brazilian Coalition / WRI Brasil report evaluating 40 economic models of native-species reforestation in Brazil — silviculture, agroforestry systems, and crop-livestock-forest integration — and finding 32 of 40 models have ROI between 9.5% and 28.4%/year.

docs.title: The Restoration Diagnostic
docs.file_name: WRI_Restoration_Diagnostic_1 (1).pdf
docs.short_summary: The Restoration Diagnostic — a foundational 2015 WRI methodology for rapidly assessing the status of key success factors for forest landscape restoration in a given area, organized as a three-step diagnostic with strategy guidance for missing factors.

docs.title: Restoring Trees to the Landscape: Creating "Shovel-Ready" Jobs across the United States
docs.file_name: expert-note-tree-restoration.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 2020 WRI COVID-19 Special Expert Note arguing that an annual federal investment of $4–4.5 billion in US tree restoration could create 150,000+ jobs and $6–12 billion in annual economic activity, while restoring up to 60 billion trees over 20 years.

docs.title: Financing Sustainable Watershed Management in Ethiopia Through Nature-Based Solutions
docs.file_name: financing-sustainable-watershed-management-ethiopia-nature-based-solutions.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI working paper assessing the applicability of innovative financing mechanisms (PES, blended finance, water funds, green bonds) to nature-based watershed management in Ethiopia, given persistent land and water degradation despite a long restoration history.

docs.title: Growing Resilience: Unlocking the Potential of Nature-Based Solutions for Climate Resilience in Sub-Saharan Africa
docs.file_name: growing-resilience-version-2.pdf
docs.short_summary: A 2025 World Bank / WRI / AfDB joint report (version 2) on unlocking nature-based solutions for climate resilience in Sub-Saharan Africa: analyzes MDB project data, surveys financing instruments, and provides recommendations for scaling NbS investment.

docs.title: Land Restoration from Planting to Proof: A Guide to Monitoring, Reporting, and Verification
docs.file_name: land-restoration-from-planting-to-proof.pdf
docs.short_summary: WRI's 2026 flagship guidebook on monitoring, reporting, and verification (MRV) for land restoration projects — built from the TerraMatch / Restore Local experience funding and tracking ~200 African restoration champions since 2022.

docs.title: Transfer Learning to Detect Natural, Monoculture, and Agroforestry Tree-Based Systems in Ghana Using Remote Sensing
docs.file_name: transfer-learning-to-detect-natural-monoculture-and-agroforestry-tree-based-systems-in-ghana-using-remote-sensing.pdf
docs.short_summary: A WRI Technical Note presenting a transfer-learning approach using a CNN to differentiate natural, monoculture, and agroforestry tree-based systems in Ghana from remote sensing imagery — addressing a label-scarce classification problem.

docs.title: A Status Report on Assisted Natural Regeneration: What, Why, Who, Where, and How?
docs.file_name: wri-anr-alliance-report-white-paper-2024.pdf
docs.short_summary: The ANR Alliance's foundational status report on Assisted Natural Regeneration — a comprehensive synthesis of what ANR is, its socio-economic and ecological goals, who practices it, where the best opportunities exist, and what policies, costs, and enabling factors support it.
